transformation_type: base-promoted halogenation and cleavage of methyl ketones or methyl carbinols to haloforms and carboxylates
mechanism_type: sequential alpha-halogenation to trihalomethyl ketones followed by hydrolytic C-C bond cleavage
reactant_pattern_text: methyl ketone or methyl carbinol + halogen source + base
common_conditions: aqueous or mixed solvent conditions with hypohalite or halogen/base combinations, often followed by acidic workup
description_short: One-carbon degradation method that converts methyl ketones and related methyl carbinols into haloforms and carboxylate products under hypohalite conditions.

transformation_type: rearrangement of activated hydroxamic acids to isocyanates and trapped amine/urea/carbamate products
mechanism_type: base- or heat-induced migration from O-acyl or O-aryl hydroxamates to isocyanates
reactant_pattern_text: O-acyl or O-aryl hydroxamic acid derivative
common_conditions: activation of hydroxamic acid followed by heating or base treatment and nucleophilic trapping of the isocyanate
description_short: Activated-hydroxamate rearrangement that furnishes isocyanates under mild conditions and enables access to amines, carbamates, and ureas after trapping.

transformation_type: CeCl3/NaBH4 reduction of alpha,beta-unsaturated ketones to allylic alcohols
mechanism_type: Lewis-acid-assisted 1,2-hydride delivery to enones with suppression of conjugate reduction
common_conditions: low temperature to room temperature in alcohol solvent with CeCl3 and NaBH4
description_short: Selective 1,2-reduction of enones to allylic alcohols using sodium borohydride in the presence of cerium(III) chloride.

transformation_type: base-promoted cyclization of N-acyl-o-toluidines or related anilides to indoles
mechanism_type: deprotonation alpha to the amide followed by intramolecular cyclization and aromatization; Smith-modified variants use Peterson olefination logic
common_conditions: high-temperature alkoxide or sodamide conditions or low-temperature Smith-modified organolithium variants
synonym_names: Madelung indole synthesis|Smith indole synthesis|Smith-modified Madelung indole synthesis
description_short: Classical and modified intramolecular anilide cyclization methods for building 2-substituted and 2,3-disubstituted indoles.

transformation_type: alkylation of malonic esters followed by hydrolysis and decarboxylation to substituted acetic acids
mechanism_type: enolate alkylation of malonate esters with electrophiles followed by saponification and decarboxylation
product_pattern_text: mono- or dialkylated acetic acid|cycloalkane derivative
common_conditions: base-promoted mono- or dialkylation followed by hydrolysis and heating with acid or base for decarboxylation
description_short: Foundational active-methylene strategy that converts alkylated malonate esters into substituted acetic acids and related cyclic products.
